commands.author: kesslerio
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=78092c6cb4696ac070c8b88a7428c5103c5fb672)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a raw `current` shortcut to `resolveCurrentSessionClientAlias` and updates resolver tests so `sessionKey: "current"` resolves to the requester without calling `sessions.resolve`.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests -51. Total -44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main still lets raw `current` reach `sessions.resolve` in the wrapper path, and the linked issue includes live logs. I did not run a live gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor revision is needed because the PR has a concrete session-routing regression and the real-behavior proof gate is still mock/static only.

target.title: fix(telegram): bind bot mentions to assistant identity
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Telegram group/topic turns where an explicit @bot-handle mention could still be interpreted as addressing someone other than the current assistant, allowing the run to execute tools and then end as `NO_REPLY`. The patch carries explicit bot mention metadata into shared inbound context and adds a Telegram-only prompt line binding the configured bot handle to assistant identity.

This PR is intentionally scoped to the bot-handle identity slice. It does not claim to close the broader linked Telegram reply-loss cluster.

## Linked context

Related openclaw/openclaw#85654. Live before-fix repro evidence: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85654#issuecomment-4703305519.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram forum-topic messages explicitly addressed to the bot handle are represented to the model as addressing the current assistant.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, executing OpenClaw source functions through Node/tsx. Live AlphaClaw/OpenClaw 2026.6.6 before-fix production repro is linked above.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` executed `buildChannelInboundEventContext(...)` and `buildGroupChatContext(...)` from this patched checkout with Telegram provider, bot username `kesslerAIBot`, explicit bot mention facts, and require-mention group policy.
- Evidence after fix: copied console output from the patched OpenClaw checkout: `{"ExplicitlyMentionedBot":true,"MentionSource":"explicit_bot","WasMentioned":true,"promptContainsBotIdentity":true,"promptContainsAddressedToYou":true}`.
- Observed result after fix: the patched branch carries explicit Telegram bot-handle mention facts into the generated turn context and binds the configured Telegram bot handle to the current assistant identity in group prompts. Non-Telegram group prompts remain unchanged.
- What was not tested: redacted after-fix live Telegram Desktop/OpenClaw visible-reply proof is still pending. I requested Mantis proof in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93088#issuecomment-4703736156, but the issue-comment workflow skipped because GitHub reports my account has read permission on this repository and Mantis requires write/maintain/admin.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm docs:list`

## Risk checklist

User-visible behavior changed: Yes, only for Telegram prompts with explicit bot-handle mentions.
Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: No.
Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
Highest-risk area: prompt wording in group chats. Mitigation: Telegram-gated prompt text, structured mention facts, retained `NO_REPLY` policy, and tests for explicit bot mentions versus non-Telegram behavior.

## Current review state

Code and local validation are ready. Live Telegram Desktop proof remains blocked on maintainer-supplied redacted Telegram Desktop/OpenClaw proof or an explicit maintainer proof override.

---
